quotes.text: The sense of danger must not disappear: The way is certainly both short and steep, However gradual it looks from here; Look if you like, but you will have to leap.
quotes.author: W.H. Auden
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: How luck works, why improvement is hard, and a call for courage

quotes.text: The number of hours we have together is actually not so large. Please linger near the door uncomfortably instead of just leaving. Please forget your scarf in my life and come back later for it.
quotes.author: Mikko Harvey
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: How luck works, why improvement is hard, and a call for courage

quotes.text: Why is improvement hard? Part of the issue is everyone wants to improve, but nobody wants to destroy. Change often requires destruction. Or, at least, unlearning. Let's call it gentle elimination. You may have to leave little habits, update current beliefs, eliminate comfortable patterns. When you want better outcomes, your daily norms may need to change. The process of improvement is not just about adding things you like. Sometimes habits and patterns belong to who you were, not who you are trying to be. If you'd like something better, then a routine you are comfortable with may have to die.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: How luck works, why improvement is hard, and a call for courage

quotes.text: What we call luck is often not purely random, but rather the delayed byproduct of consistently putting yourself in good positions.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: How luck works, why improvement is hard, and a call for courage

quotes.text: Talent is a gift. Reliability is a choice. But every family, team, and organization needs reliability just as much as they need talent.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: How luck works, why improvement is hard, and a call for courage

quotes.text: If we have the same assets and I can earn an 8% annual returns, and you can earn 12% annual returns, but I need half as much money to be happy while your lifestyle compounds as fast as your assets, I'm better off than you are. I'm getting more benefit from my investments despite lower returns. Think about that in the context of how much time and effort goes into achieving 0.1% of annual outperformance in this business – millions of hours of research, tens of billions of dollars of effort – and it's easy to see what's potentially more important or worth chasing. There are investors who grind 80 hours a week to add 10 basis points to their returns when there are two or three full percentage points of lifestyle bloat in their finances that can be exploited with less effort. Outperformance is amazing when it can be achieved, and some can achieve a lot of it. But the fact that there's so much effort put into one side of the finance equation and so little put into the other is an opportunity for most people, companies, and countries. This is not about living like a monk, hampered by frugality. It holds true at every level of wealth and spending. The value of all money is relative to expectations... The idea that reducing your needs has the same impact as increasing your income – but the former is more certain and in your control than the latter, so it has a higher expected value – is as true for someone spending $15,000 a year as it is someone spending $15 million per year.
quotes.author: Morgan Housel
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On improving the world, feeling wealthy, and managing your three selves

quotes.text: Childhood may be defined as the age of play; therefore some children are never young, and some adults are never old.
quotes.author: Will Durant
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On improving the world, feeling wealthy, and managing your three selves

quotes.text: Be forgiving with your past self. What's done is done. No sense in beating yourself up about it. Be strict with your present self. Win the moment in front of you right now. Be flexible with your future self. There are many paths to success. You don't need life to be a certain way to live well.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On improving the world, feeling wealthy, and managing your three selves

quotes.text: If you are bummed by the state of the world, go build something.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On improving the world, feeling wealthy, and managing your three selves

quotes.text: You don't need to solve every problem right now, only those that stand in your way.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On improving the world, feeling wealthy, and managing your three selves

quotes.text: I believe that if, at the end of it all, according to our abilities, we have done something to make others a little happier, and something to make ourselves a little happier, that is about the best we can do... We must try to contribute joy to the world. That is true no matter what our problems, our health, our circumstances. We must try. I didn’t always know this, and am happy I lived long enough to find it out.
quotes.author: Roger Ebert
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On responding to challenges, winning the day, and lessons from a baseball coach

quotes.text: Show up every day. Work hard. Do it with joy. Care more about others than we do ourselves.
quotes.author: Mike Deegan
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On responding to challenges, winning the day, and lessons from a baseball coach

quotes.text: A lovely blend: Always reaching. Already enough.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On responding to challenges, winning the day, and lessons from a baseball coach

quotes.text: Compete against yourself. When you look outside — your rivals, your industry, your luck — there is always something to blame. When you look inside — your process, your effort, your rate of learning — there is always something to improve. Average looks out. Elite looks in.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On responding to challenges, winning the day, and lessons from a baseball coach

quotes.text: Your response to a challenge defines you more than the challenge itself.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On responding to challenges, winning the day, and lessons from a baseball coach

quotes.text: Until one is committed there is hesitancy, the chance to draw back, always ineffectiveness. Concerning all acts of initiative (and creation), there is one elementary truth, the ignorance of which kills countless ideas and splendid plans: that the moment one definitely commits oneself, then Providence moves too. All sorts of things occur to help one that would never otherwise have occurred. A whole stream of events issues from the decision, raising in one's favour all manner of unforeseen incidents and meetings and material assistance, which no man could have dreamt would have come his way. I have learned a deep respect for one of Goethe's couplets: Whatever you can do, or dream you can, begin it. Boldness has genius, power, and magic in it.
quotes.author: W. H. Murray
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On revenge, three principles to live by, and the power of commitment

quotes.text: The best revenge is not to be like that.
quotes.author: Marcus Aurelius
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On revenge, three principles to live by, and the power of commitment

quotes.text: I try to live to a standard, not the circumstances of the day. Some days it is easy. Some days it is hard. But I do it either way because that's who I want to be, not because it is always easy to do.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On revenge, three principles to live by, and the power of commitment

quotes.text: It's not my job to tell myself no. The world will offer plenty of obstacles. I don't need to add to them. My mind is my friend. I am my own biggest believer.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On revenge, three principles to live by, and the power of commitment

quotes.text: I'm adaptable. I don't need the day to go perfectly to make it a good one.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On revenge, three principles to live by, and the power of commitment

quotes.text: What can be added to the happiness of a person who is in health, out of debt, and has a clear conscience?
quotes.author: Adam Smith
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On making mistakes, the paradox of creating, and why habits translate

quotes.text: Only while sleeping one makes no mistakes. Making mistakes is the privilege of the active — of those who can correct their mistakes and put them right.
quotes.author: Ingvar Kamprad
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On making mistakes, the paradox of creating, and why habits translate

quotes.text: I recently found this note to myself: No sense in thinking small. Don't water down your vision. A remarkable amount can be accomplished if you are willing to think longer term than most and work hard each day.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On making mistakes, the paradox of creating, and why habits translate

quotes.text: When you are creating something, you will usually find that once things appear 90 percent done, you are actually about halfway there. But you have to create the first 90 percent in order to see the half that needs to be revised.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On making mistakes, the paradox of creating, and why habits translate

quotes.text: When you lose a game, the score doesn't transfer to the next contest but your habits certainly will. Circumstances are temporary. Sometimes you're winning, sometimes you're losing. Hot, cold. Lucky, unlucky. But your habits travel with you. This is why you want to execute the same way whether the score is 10-0 or 0-10. Not because the score doesn't matter, but because the score isn't what you're actually building. It's not about winning or losing any given round. It's about doing things the right way. If you have a chance to practice your craft, you want to do it as well as you can (even if you end up losing that day). Your previous reps can save you or betray you. The habits always translate to the next round.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On making mistakes, the paradox of creating, and why habits translate

quotes.text: Often we fail to improve our lives simply because things don't get bad enough. If your new job is hell, you’ll leave it, but if it’s just unsatisfying, you’ll likely grind it out. Thus, small problems often threaten our quality of life more than big ones.
quotes.author: Gurwinder Bhogal
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On the power of self-image, how luck travels, and the importance of solving mediocre problems

quotes.text: No matter how busy you are, you must take time to make the other person feel important. Everyone has an invisible sign hanging from their neck saying, "Make me feel important." Never forget this message when working with people.
quotes.author: Mary Kay Ash
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On the power of self-image, how luck travels, and the importance of solving mediocre problems

quotes.text: You will rarely outperform your self-image.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On the power of self-image, how luck travels, and the importance of solving mediocre problems

quotes.text: Luck flows through people and travels by conversation. The people you talk to determine the opportunities you find. Keep talking to the same people, keep finding the same opportunities. Start talking to new people, start finding new opportunities. If you want different luck, start walking into different rooms.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On the power of self-image, how luck travels, and the importance of solving mediocre problems

quotes.text: The best view of the game is probably from the stands. But that's not where the action is. And so you have to decide, do you want a nice view or do you want to be in the thick of it and playing the game?
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On the power of self-image, how luck travels, and the importance of solving mediocre problems

quotes.text: If you only wished to be happy, this could be easily accomplished; but we wish to be happier than other people, and this is always difficult, for we believe others to be happier than they are.
quotes.author: Montesquieu
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On living a disciplined life, three types of relationships, and how to be happy

quotes.text: No one tells you running is 10 percent cardio and 90 percent negotiating with the voice begging you to quit.
quotes.author: David Dack
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On living a disciplined life, three types of relationships, and how to be happy

quotes.text: Three ways to think about relationships: * Deepening. You know each other better this year than last. * Holding. Steady state, but mostly comfortable, familiar, and unchanged. * Drifting. Contact is fading and the relationship is starting to slide. The point isn't to judge where you're at, but to notice what you need next. Which relationship is due for a deepening period? Which one are you okay letting drift for now?
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On living a disciplined life, three types of relationships, and how to be happy

quotes.text: The most expensive part of a bad habit is rarely the action itself, but rather the identity it quietly reinforces underneath. The issue isn't skipping once. It's that you practiced being someone who skips. Now, that doesn't mean you need to be perfect. Everyone falls off from time to time. But it is good to have a plan for getting back on track quickly. And try to find a small way to practice being the type of person you want to be today.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On living a disciplined life, three types of relationships, and how to be happy

quotes.text: The disciplined life looks impressive, but the well-designed life lasts longer. Before you try to find more willpower, design your environment and arrange your day so you're more likely to follow through.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On living a disciplined life, three types of relationships, and how to be happy

quotes.text: A frog's brain is set up to recognize food as moving objects that are oblong in shape. So if we take a frog's normal food -- flies -- paralyze them with a little chloroform and put them in front of the frog, it will not notice them or try to eat them. It will starve in front of its food! But if we throw little rectangular pieces of cardboard at the frog it will eat them until it is stuffed! The frog only sees a little of the world we see, but it still thinks it perceives the whole world. Now, of course, we are not like frogs! Or are we? talk, Kay says, "You can't learn to see until you realize you are blind.
quotes.author: Alan Kay
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On making adjustments, how to handle criticism, and the secret to improvement

quotes.text: Teach in order to learn.
quotes.author: Baba Hari Dass
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On making adjustments, how to handle criticism, and the secret to improvement

quotes.text: With a bow and arrow, you aim before you shoot. But in most areas of life, aiming is something you can do throughout the process. You can always adjust: your career path, your business strategy, your relationships, your workout program, your plans for next Wednesday. It's all adjustable along the way. So, pick a direction and get moving. Once you start, you learn along the way and there are plenty of opportunities to refine your plan.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On making adjustments, how to handle criticism, and the secret to improvement

quotes.text: You can take things seriously without taking them personally. Our tendency is to turn any criticism or complaint into a personal attack. We reply to it, defend against it, build a counter-argument, lose sleep over it. You don't have to eat everything that is served to you. You can respond to criticism without digesting criticism. Take what's useful, do your best to improve, and leave the rest.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On making adjustments, how to handle criticism, and the secret to improvement

quotes.text: Improvement is being better than your past self. It doesn't have to be more complicated than that. Do not compare against others, compare against your past self. Keep the focus internal.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On making adjustments, how to handle criticism, and the secret to improvement

quotes.text: Champions behave like champions before they're champions; they have a winning standard of performance before they are winners.
quotes.author: Bill Walsh
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On acting like a winner, thinking for yourself, and how to stop chasing what isn't working

quotes.text: You don't have to make it back the way that you lost it.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On acting like a winner, thinking for yourself, and how to stop chasing what isn't working

quotes.text: It's not a race. You are not ahead. You are not behind. You are here. Enjoy it and make the most of it.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On acting like a winner, thinking for yourself, and how to stop chasing what isn't working

quotes.text: So much of what we believe is the residue of someone else's thinking. Pause and question things for a moment. Is this really how it has to be? Is this really what you want?
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On acting like a winner, thinking for yourself, and how to stop chasing what isn't working

quotes.text: Big outcome: Do the right thing well. Big lesson: Do the right thing poorly. Big risk: Do the wrong thing well.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On acting like a winner, thinking for yourself, and how to stop chasing what isn't working

quotes.text: A good reputation is acquired by many actions; and can be lost by one. Be upon your guard, therefore, against those weaknesses which may risk it. Nothing can be more unjust than to judge a person by one single action; but the world is seldom just.
quotes.author: Chesterfield
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On clarity of purpose, the skill of noticing, and what it takes to build a reputation

quotes.text: If you do not know which port you are seeking, no wind is favorable.
quotes.author: Seneca
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On clarity of purpose, the skill of noticing, and what it takes to build a reputation

quotes.text: Observation is a skill, and like any skill, it can be trained and honed. Even if you're not a negative person, you may be skilled at noticing negative things. Sometimes people are good at noticing the reason things won't work out or have a tendency to fixate on the latest distressing story. But you can train your eye toward the opportunities each day quietly presents. You can become competent at noticing your good luck: the little moments of joy, the stranger who helped, the small things that went right, the opportunity in front of you right now. What are you competent in observing? And which types of observations seem to serve your life best?
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On clarity of purpose, the skill of noticing, and what it takes to build a reputation

quotes.text: An interesting phrase I overheard recently: I work harder, but you're busier.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On clarity of purpose, the skill of noticing, and what it takes to build a reputation

quotes.text: Challenge yourself when life is easy, so you'll be ready when life is hard.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On clarity of purpose, the skill of noticing, and what it takes to build a reputation

quotes.text: I am Me, and I hope to become Me more and more.
quotes.author: Paula Modersohn-Becker
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On ignorance vs. genius, the history of every day, and the process of becoming yourself

quotes.text: The history of every day. — What is the history of every day in your case? Look at your habits that constitute it: are they the product of innumerable little cowardices and lazinesses, or of your courage and inventive reason?
quotes.author: and writer Friedrich Nietzsche
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On ignorance vs. genius, the history of every day, and the process of becoming yourself

quotes.text: Work is endless. Exercise is endless. Parenting is endless. Same with marriage, writing, investing, creating, and more. You get to choose the parts of your life, but many of the important things in life cannot be "finished. Do not approach an endless game with a finite mindset. The objective is not to be done, but to settle into a daily lifestyle you can sustain and that allows you to make daily progress on the areas that matter. Embrace the fact that life is continual and look for ways to enjoy the daily practice.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On ignorance vs. genius, the history of every day, and the process of becoming yourself

quotes.text: Success is often found by practicing the fundamentals everyone knows they should be doing, but find too boring or basic to practice routinely.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On ignorance vs. genius, the history of every day, and the process of becoming yourself

quotes.text: To simplify before you understand the details is ignorance. To simplify after you understand the details is genius.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On ignorance vs. genius, the history of every day, and the process of becoming yourself

quotes.text: The truest form of intelligence is designing the life you want to live.
quotes.author: Graham Weaver
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On the ultimate form of preparation, how to increase your power, and living with conviction

quotes.text: A dead thing can go with the stream, but only a living thing can go against it.
quotes.author: G. K. Chesterton
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On the ultimate form of preparation, how to increase your power, and living with conviction

quotes.text: The more you create, the more powerful you become. The more you consume, the more powerful others become.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On the ultimate form of preparation, how to increase your power, and living with conviction

quotes.text: The ultimate form of preparation is not planning for a specific scenario, but a mindset that can handle uncertainty.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On the ultimate form of preparation, how to increase your power, and living with conviction

quotes.text: Compete externally and you compare. Compete internally and you improve.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On the ultimate form of preparation, how to increase your power, and living with conviction

quotes.text: When you are content to be simply yourself and don't compare or compete, everybody will respect you. translation reads closer to something like, "Because he doesn't compare himself with others, no one can compare with him.
quotes.author: Lao Tzu
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On regret, how to have your best ideas, and the key to respect

quotes.text: How picturesque do those trains later seem to us that we failed to catch.
quotes.author: Jules Laforgue
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On regret, how to have your best ideas, and the key to respect

quotes.text: It is nearly impossible to have your best idea the first time you think about something. The most likely way to uncover important insights is to frequently revisit a problem. The longer you're in the game, the more ideas bubble up to the surface. Time unlocks insights.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On regret, how to have your best ideas, and the key to respect

quotes.text: The amateur does not know what to do. The master knows what not to do.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On regret, how to have your best ideas, and the key to respect

quotes.text: To learn, wander. To achieve, focus.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On regret, how to have your best ideas, and the key to respect

quotes.text: Arrange whatever pieces come your way.
quotes.author: Virginia Woolf
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On finding your desired lifestyle, a simple rule for life, and working with what you have

quotes.text: Well begun is half done.
quotes.author: Horace
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On finding your desired lifestyle, a simple rule for life, and working with what you have

quotes.text: Here's a rule I find useful: You should attempt things that are difficult enough to guarantee some early embarrassment, but important enough that long-term regret is unlikely. Trying something difficult will usually make you look foolish or inexperienced. That’s fine. That's the cost of learning. But if it's important to you, then you'll work through the early failures and — even if things ultimately change shape or don't work out — you'll never regret going for it.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On finding your desired lifestyle, a simple rule for life, and working with what you have

quotes.text: Reflection requires stillness. One cost of rushing from thing to thing is that you lose the space to think. Hard work matters, but nonstop motion often hides a quiet truth: you could have used your time better. If you never pause, you confuse activity with effectiveness. Make time to think. Walk outside. Sit quietly. Create space. Then move again, but this time on purpose.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On finding your desired lifestyle, a simple rule for life, and working with what you have

quotes.text: Optimize for your desired lifestyle, not your desired title.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On finding your desired lifestyle, a simple rule for life, and working with what you have

quotes.text: Do not merely think that you are going to become great; think that you are great now. Do not think that you will begin to act in a great way at some future time; begin now. Do not think that you will act in a great way when you reach a different environment; act in a great way where you are now. Do not think that you will begin to act in a great way when you begin to deal with great things; begin to deal in a great way with small things.
quotes.author: Wallace Wattles
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: Being great today, what to do when things don't go well, and favorite type of mental toughness

quotes.text: What are you still carrying that isn't yours and is weighing you down?
quotes.author: interior designer Rita Wilkins
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: Being great today, what to do when things don't go well, and favorite type of mental toughness

quotes.text: Earlier this week I read a line that stood out to me, "Your goal in life should be to reduce the amount of time it takes you to get out of a bad state. The ability to bounce back quickly is a key skill in life. My wife once told me, "When you're five, you can be mad for a day. When you're ten, you can be mad for an hour. By the time you're thirty, you get ten minutes—and then you have to move on. Life is full of moments of frustration and disappointment. Growth and maturity is learning to pull yourself out of a bad state faster.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: Being great today, what to do when things don't go well, and favorite type of mental toughness

quotes.text: My favorite type of mental toughness is not forcing one path, but being open to many paths: Whatever comes my way, I can handle it. Whatever resources I have, I can make it work. Whatever the day brings, I can thrive.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: Being great today, what to do when things don't go well, and favorite type of mental toughness

quotes.text: When things don't go well it's easy to wonder, "Why me?" It's easy to point fingers. It's easy to wallow in frustration or defeat. But it is also easy to ask, "What is this teaching me? You can't remove the frustrations from life, but you can always try to come out a little wiser on the other side.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: Being great today, what to do when things don't go well, and favorite type of mental toughness

quotes.text: Art is limitation; the essence of every picture is the frame.
quotes.author: Philosopher
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On balancing gratitude and ambition, the best things in life, and feeling like you have enough

quotes.text: Love doesn't just sit there, like a stone, it has to be made, like bread; remade all the time, made new.
quotes.author: Ursula K. Le Guin
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On balancing gratitude and ambition, the best things in life, and feeling like you have enough

quotes.text: Many of the best things in life are endless. Being in a great relationship. Staying fit and healthy. Doing work that fulfills you. Being a good parent, coach, or teacher. Stop worrying about accomplishing these things and instead focus on building a life where you continually practice them. The important stuff has no finish line.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On balancing gratitude and ambition, the best things in life, and feeling like you have enough

quotes.text: Do not fear what you can address. What should worry you is not the problem, but if you are resisting addressing it.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On balancing gratitude and ambition, the best things in life, and feeling like you have enough

quotes.text: Each day, spend some time on two things: * working toward something that will pay off years from now * appreciating something that is happening right now
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: On balancing gratitude and ambition, the best things in life, and feeling like you have enough

quotes.text: I want to stay as close to the edge as I can without going over. Out on the edge you see all kinds of things you can't see from the center. Big, undreamed‑of things—the people on the edge see them first.
quotes.author: Kurt Vonnegut teaches us
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: A simple practice for peace, how to become disciplined, and being bold

quotes.text: Don't explain your philosophy. Embody it.
quotes.author: Epictetus
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: A simple practice for peace, how to become disciplined, and being bold

quotes.text: Pause and try this: Breathe in, and close your eyes. Breathe out, and smile.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: A simple practice for peace, how to become disciplined, and being bold

quotes.text: Your thoughts and actions belong to you just like your possessions. Every so often, it helps to declutter—donate old clothes or clear out a crowded shelf. Maybe it's time to let go of some unhelpful thoughts or outdated actions too?
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: A simple practice for peace, how to become disciplined, and being bold

quotes.text: Intelligence isn't just about what you know. It is also the ability to avoid being your own bottleneck. * If you lack the skills, be willing to look foolish while you learn them. * If you lack the connections, be courageous enough to reach out and build them. * If you feel uncertain, be bold enough to figure it out along the way. Many people have the ability, but they talk themselves out of trying.
quotes.author: James Clear
quotes.email_subject: 3-2-1: A simple practice for peace, how to become disciplined, and being bold

questions.text: Observing greatness can inspire us to be great ourselves. Noticing high-quality work can motivate us to improve our craft. What do you keep near you because it is beautiful?

questions.text: Don't wait on external factors. If the thing you're waiting for never arrives or if the permission you're hoping for is never given, what's your move?
